metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_586
metadata.comment:
Rationale: The paper tests the effect of SCAMP5 knock-down on SV recycling and endocytosis. Rat hippocampal neurons were cotransfected with vGpH (a vesicular glutamate transporter-1 fused with pHluorin), and shRNA against SCAMP5.
Figure 3 describes the endocytosis of SVs following 300 APs at 10 Hz, as monitored by following the vGpH signal, and demonstrates that endocytosis is slowed in the knock-down neurons. The effect can be rescued by the re-expression of SCAMP5.
Figure 4 shows that the knock-down of SCAMP5 impairs endocytosis during stimulation. The neurons are stimulated by an AP train (300 APs at 10 Hz) to evaluate exocytosis, and, following recovery, by a train of 1200 APs at 10 Hz in the presence of Bafilomycin A1 (blocking SV reacidification following exocytosis), to evaluate the size of the recycling SV pool. This experiment demonstrates that the ratio of Endocytosis to exocytosis is lower in SCAMP5 KD neurons, suggesting that the fraction of the released SVs that undergoes endocytosis during the stimulation is lower than in the WT controls. The effect was fully rescued by the re-expression of SCAMP5.. Experimental description: Neuron culture and transfection. Hippocampal neurons derived from
embryonic day 18 Sprague Dawley fetal rats of either sex were prepared as
described previously
